name: Laserfiche ECM & Process Automation API
what_it_does: Laserfiche REST API for enterprise content management and business process automation platform. Enables AI agents to manage document repository operations including storage, retrieval, search, and metadata management for enterprise content governance, handle process automation and workflow routing via Laserfiche Forms and Workflow for business process automation, access records management and retention scheduling data for regulatory compliance, retrieve Laserfiche Forms submission and process tracking data for workflow analytics, handle document template and generation automation for standardized document production, access audit trail and user activity data for compliance monitoring, retrieve repository folder structure and security permission data for access governance, manage document metadata and field tagging for content classification, handle public portal and citizen-facing form submission data for government digital services, and integrate document management with ERP, line-of-business, and citizen service platforms.
use_cases: Automating document intake from business process agents
Retrieving records for compliance from information governance agents
Processing form submissions from digital government service agents
Managing document workflows from approval routing agents
not_for: Cloud-native document management without enterprise on-premise ECM needs
Enterprise-scale content requiring Hyland or OpenText complexity
Consumer document storage without institutional records management
best_when: A government agency, school district, financial institution, or mid-to-large enterprise using Laserfiche wants AI agents to automate document intake, records management, process workflows, forms processing, and citizen service delivery.
avoid_when: COMPLIANCE RISK: Laserfiche manages government and regulated records — automated records disposition without retention schedule validation creates legal hold and regulatory recordkeeping violations. Government agency Laserfiche deployments may contain sensitive and confidential public records; automated access and sharing requires public records law and security classification review. Process automation that bypasses approval controls creates audit and segregation of duties failures.

auth.notes: Laserfiche uses OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code flow). Developer portal at developer.laserfiche.com. Long Beach, California HQ. Private (Compulink Management Center, Inc.). Laserfiche Cloud is the primary SaaS product; Laserfiche Self-Hosted also supported. Strong in government (city, county, state), education, financial services. Developer-friendly REST API with good documentation. Competes with Hyland OnBase and OpenText for mid-market ECM.

pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per named user; module pricing for Forms, Workflow, Audit Trail
pricing.notes: Long Beach, California. Founded 1987. Private (Compulink Management Center). 40,000+ organizations globally. Strong in government, education, and financial services. Laserfiche Cloud (SaaS), Laserfiche Self-Hosted options. Annual Empower Conference. Laserfiche Marketplace for add-on integrations. Competes with Hyland OnBase for government and education ECM; also competes with Microsoft SharePoint.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; plan dependent

known_agent_gotchas: COMPLIANCE RISK: Laserfiche records lifecycle automation must comply with retention schedules; automated disposition without retention manager review creates destruction of regulated records

Government public records law — government agency Laserfiche deployments handle public records subject to FOIA, state open records laws; automated record sharing or deletion must comply with public records requirements
Self-hosted vs. cloud API differences — Laserfiche Self-Hosted has different API endpoints and may have version-specific feature differences from Laserfiche Cloud; verify deployment type
Workflow scripting integration — Laserfiche Workflow has SDK scripting for complex automation; REST API covers CRUD operations but complex workflow routing may require Workflow SDK
Document security permissions — Laserfiche has granular per-document and per-folder security; automated document creation must apply correct security template to prevent unauthorized access
Forms and repository integration — Laserfiche Forms submissions link to repository documents; ensure form-repository integration is correctly configured for automated process flows
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise content management. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, FedRAMP. OAuth2. US/EU. Government and enterprise records data.

security_score_components.security_notes: ECM and records management. SOC2, FedRAMP, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Government and enterprise records.
